docs.title: Project Showcase Skill
docs.text: Automate the "last mile" of development — generate visual galleries, audit repo health, inject professional READMEs, and launch on social media.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text: Script Purpose scripts/capture.py Playwright-based UI screenshots (desktop, tablet, mobile) scripts/scan.py Security scan for hardcoded secrets and .env leaks scripts/audit.py Repo health scoring (0-100) with healing plan scripts/inject_readme.py Surgical README gallery injection scripts/release.py GitHub release creation with auto-changelog scripts/manage_metadata.py Auto-detect and update GitHub topics/description scripts/setup.sh One-shot dependency installer

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# 1. Install the skill
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ayushxx7/project-showcase-skill/main/skills.sh | bash

# 2. Setup dependencies
./scripts/setup.sh

# 3. Run a health audit
python3 scripts/audit.py --dir /path/to/your/project --heal

# 4. Capture UI
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --responsive

# 5. Scan for secrets
python3 scripts/scan.py --dir /path/to/your/project

docs.title: Agent Commands
docs.text:
When installed as a skill, tell your agent:

  • "Showcase this project" — Full pipeline: capture + audit + README + release
  • "Audit this project" — Health score + healing plan
  • "Capture the UI" — Screenshots across viewports
  • "Scan for secrets" — Pre-publish security check
  • "Update the README" — Surgical gallery injection
  • "Create a release" — GitHub release with changelog
  • "Launch on LinkedIn" — Post templates + asset bundling

docs.title: Sub-Skills
docs.text: • LinkedIn Magic (linkedin-magic/) — Post templates, asset bundling, hashtag strategy

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT — see LICENSE

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions welcome — bug fixes, features, docs improvements.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/ayushxx7/project-showcase-skill
cd project-showcase-skill
./scripts/setup.sh

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: python3 -m pytest tests/

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Branch from main
  2. Add tests for new logic
  3. Update docs if adding/changing features
  4. Open a PR

docs.title: 1. Install the Skill
docs.text:
Add it to your AI agent using the universal installer:

curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ayushxx7/project-showcase-skill/main/skills.sh | bash

This detects and installs into: - Gemini CLI (~/.gemini/skills/) - Claude Code (~/.claude/skills/) - Hermes Agent (~/.hermes/profiles/*/skills/) - Generic agents (~/.agents/skills/)

docs.title: 2. Setup Dependencies
docs.text:
./scripts/setup.sh

This installs system deps (VHS, ffmpeg) and Python packages (Playwright + Chromium).

docs.title: 3. Verify
docs.text:
python3 scripts/audit.py --dir . --heal
python3 scripts/scan.py --dir .
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:8501 --responsive

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Tool Purpose Install Python 3.8+ Scripts runtime python.org Playwright UI capture pip install playwright && playwright install chromium ffmpeg Video/GIF processing brew install ffmpeg (macOS)

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: Tool Purpose Install VHS Terminal GIF recording brew install vhs (macOS) GitHub CLI (gh) Releases, metadata cli.github.com MkDocs Material Local docs site pip install mkdocs-material

docs.title: Run the Docs Site Locally
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

docs.title: LinkedIn Launch Guide
docs.text: Transform your project into LinkedIn engagement using the linkedin-magic sub-skill.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Prep — Run the main showcase skill first (capture + audit + README)
  2. Generate — Pick a post variant (see below)
  3. Bundle — Run linkedin-magic/scripts/bundle_assets.py to prepare images
  4. Post — Use the generated content + assets

docs.title: 🚀 The Viral Hype (Short & Punchy)
docs.text: Focus: "The Magic Pill" angle. Emojis, badges, high-conversion hooks. Best for: Projects with a clear, simple value proposition.

docs.title: 🏗️ The Deep Dive (Technical Architecture)
docs.text: Focus: "How it works." Mermaid diagrams, tech stack logic. Best for: Infrastructure, tools, frameworks.

docs.title: 📈 The Health Audit (Trust & Quality)
docs.text: Focus: Repo Health score. Proves reliability. Best for: Open-source libraries, production-ready tools.

docs.title: 💡 The Story (Problem/Solution)
docs.text: Focus: Narrative. Why you built it and the struggle it solves. Best for: Personal projects, startup tools.

docs.title: Templates
docs.text: See linkedin-magic/references/post_templates.md for fill-in-the-blank templates for each variant.

docs.title: Asset Bundling
docs.text:
python3 linkedin-magic/scripts/bundle_assets.py

This scans showcase/ and assets/ for the best images and copies them to linkedin_launch/ with descriptive names: - 01_Landing_Page.png - 02_Dashboard_View.png - 03_System_Architecture.png - 04_CLI_Demo.gif - 05_Repo_Health.png

docs.title: Hashtag Strategy
docs.text: Core: #BuildingInPublic #AIAgents #OpenSource Tech-specific: #Python #React #AI #MachineLearning Skill-specific: #ShowcaseSkill #VibeCoding

docs.title: Pro Tips
docs.text:
• First Comment Rule — Post links in the first comment, not the main post (avoids algorithm penalty)
  • Lead with visuals — LinkedIn is a visual platform. Images > text.
  • Human-first tone — Avoid robotic "AI-generated" language.

docs.title: README Customization
docs.text: The skill's README injection is designed to be surgical — it enhances without destroying.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
1. Show first, tell second — Visual gallery goes at the top, before any ## section
  2. Never duplicate — If a section exists, it's replaced or merged, not duplicated
  3. Preserve manual docs — Your hand-written content is never deleted
  4. Vibe-first — No diagnostic tables or health scores in the main README. Those go in REPO_HEALTH.md

docs.title: README Structure (Recommended)
docs.text:
# Project Title
[badges]

Elevator pitch — one sentence.

## 🎬 Showcase Gallery
[screenshots, GIFs, architecture diagrams]

## ✨ Features
[what it does]

## 🛠️ Installation
[how to set up]

## 📖 Usage
[how to use it]

## 📜 License

docs.title: Health Score Separation
docs.text:
The main README should NOT contain: - Health score tables (✅ Secure | 92/100) - Diagnostic checkmark lists - Audit percentages

Move these to REPO_HEALTH.md and link to it from the README if needed.

docs.title: Badge Guidelines
docs.text:
• "Tested on Gemini CLI" — Only for agentic projects (skills, MCP servers, agent tools)
  • "Live App" — Only if there's a deployed URL
  • License badge — Always include if LICENSE exists

docs.title: Responsive Capture
docs.text: Capture your UI across desktop, tablet, and mobile in one command.

docs.title: Viewports
docs.text: Device Dimensions Desktop 1920 x 1080 Tablet 768 x 1024 Mobile 375 x 667

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --responsive

This produces: - showcase/landing_desktop.png - showcase/landing_tablet.png - showcase/landing_mobile.png

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text:
• Portfolio quality: Shows you considered responsive design
  • Feature discovery: Some features (mobile menus) only appear on small screens
  • Visual verification: Ensures layout doesn't break across devices

docs.title: Secret Scanning
docs.text:
Before any capture or publish, run the security scanner:

python3 scripts/scan.py --dir /path/to/project

This prevents: - Hardcoded API keys in source code - .env files committed to git - Secrets visible in UI screenshots

docs.title: Visual Masking
docs.text:
If your UI displays sensitive data (emails, API keys, personal info), use masking:

python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --mask ".api-key,.user-email,#secret-field"

This hides matching elements via visibility: hidden before taking screenshots.

docs.title: License
docs.text: If a project is missing a license, the skill proposes MIT. This ensures open-source compliance.

docs.title: Health Score
docs.text: The audit (scripts/audit.py) assigns a security score based on: - No hardcoded secrets detected - .gitignore covers .env, secrets.toml, node_modules, __pycache__

docs.title: Repo Audit (audit.py)
docs.text: Scores a project's showcase readiness on a 0-100 scale.

docs.title: Scoring
docs.text: Category Max Criteria Documentation 15 README quality, LICENSE, install instructions Security 15 No secrets, proper .gitignore Automation 20 setup.sh, tests, CI/CD, dependency manifest Showcase 20 Screenshots, VHS tapes, capture.py Distribution 30 Git remote, GitHub topics, releases

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Score a project
python3 scripts/audit.py --dir /path/to/project

# Score + healing plan
python3 scripts/audit.py --dir /path/to/project --heal

# JSON output (for automation)
python3 scripts/audit.py --dir /path/to/project --json

docs.title: Healing Plan
docs.text: When run with --heal, lists all failed checks prioritized by category. Fix the ❌ items first to reach 100%.

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
The audit is automatically run as part of the /showcase agent workflow. Results can be written to REPO_HEALTH.md:

python3 scripts/audit.py --dir . > REPO_HEALTH.md

docs.title: UI Capture (capture.py)
docs.text: Playwright-based browser automation for high-quality screenshots and video.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
Use capture.py for web applications — React, Streamlit, Next.js, etc.

For CLI tools and skills, use VHS terminal recording instead (see CLI Recording).

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Responsive viewports: Desktop (1920x1080), Tablet (768x1024), Mobile (375x667)
  • Full-page screenshots: Scroll and capture entire pages
  • Visual masking: Hide sensitive elements via CSS selectors
  • Dark mode: Capture in light or dark theme
  • Video recording: .webm output converted to optimized GIF
  • Hydration awareness: Waits for networkidle and framework selectors (React, Streamlit)
  • Interaction flows: Click, fill, hover sequences with per-step screenshots

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Basic landing page capture
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000

# Responsive (desktop + tablet + mobile)
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --responsive

# With video recording
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --record-video

# Dark mode + full page
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --theme dark --full-page

# Mask sensitive elements
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --mask ".api-key,.user-email"

# Custom interactions (JSON)
python3 scripts/capture.py --url http://localhost:3000 --interactions '[{"action":"fill","selector":"#search","value":"test","filename":"search.png"}]'

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Default Description --url http://localhost:8501 Target URL --dir showcase Output directory --responsive off Capture all 3 viewports --full-page off Full scrollable page --theme light light or dark --mask — Comma-separated CSS selectors to hide --record-video off Record .webm + convert to GIF --interactions — JSON interaction sequence --hydration-delay 5 Seconds to wait for UI hydration --ab-mode off A/B comparison capture

docs.title: README Injection (inject_readme.py)
docs.text: Surgically injects showcase galleries into READMEs without destroying manual docs.

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
1. If a Showcase Gallery section exists → replace it
  2. If no gallery section → insert after the first paragraph, before the first ## heading
  3. Never duplicates — checks for existing markers
  4. Badge injection — places shields.io badges after the # title line
  5. Deduplication — warns on and removes duplicate section headings

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Inject a gallery block
python3 scripts/inject_readme.py --readme README.md --gallery "## 🎬 Showcase Gallery\n![Demo](showcase/demo.gif)"

# Inject badges
python3 scripts/inject_readme.py --readme README.md --badges "[![Live App](...)](...)"

# Check for issues without modifying
python3 scripts/inject_readme.py --readme README.md --check

# Preview changes
python3 scripts/inject_readme.py --readme README.md --gallery "..." --dry-run

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --readme Path to README (default: README.md) --gallery Gallery markdown block to inject --badges Badge markdown block to inject --check Check for duplicate sections only --dry-run Preview without writing --no-backup Skip .bak.timestamp backup

docs.title: Safety
docs.text:
• Always creates a backup before modifying (unless --no-backup)
  • Preserves all existing manual documentation
  • Idempotent — running twice won't duplicate content

docs.title: Metadata (manage_metadata.py)
docs.text: Auto-detects and updates GitHub repository metadata.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Topic detection — scans file patterns to suggest GitHub topics (e.g., requirements.txt → python)
  • Description extraction — pulls elevator pitch from README
  • Auto-apply — uses gh repo edit to update GitHub directly

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Preview suggested topics + description
python3 scripts/manage_metadata.py

# Apply to GitHub
python3 scripts/manage_metadata.py --apply

# Apply + commit + push
python3 scripts/manage_metadata.py --apply --commit --push

docs.title: Topic Detection
docs.text:
File Pattern Topics Added requirements.txt python package.json javascript, nodejs go.mod go Cargo.toml rust Dockerfile / docker-compose docker pytest / jest testing tailwind tailwindcss react react next nextjs github/workflows github-actions, automation

Base topics showcase and automation are always included.

docs.title: Scripts Overview
docs.text:
The skill is powered by 6 Python scripts and 2 shell scripts:

Script Language Purpose scripts/capture.py Python Playwright UI screenshots scripts/scan.py Python Secret leak detection scripts/audit.py Python Repo health scoring scripts/inject_readme.py Python README gallery injection scripts/release.py Python GitHub release automation scripts/manage_metadata.py Python GitHub topics/description scripts/setup.sh Bash Dependency installer skills.sh Bash Universal skill installer

All Python scripts support --help for options.

docs.title: GitHub Release (release.py)
docs.text: Creates GitHub releases with auto-generated changelogs from commit history.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Auto-versioning — suggests next semver based on commit prefixes (feat:, fix:, BREAKING)
  • Auto-changelog — groups commits by type (Features, Bug Fixes, Docs, etc.)
  • Pre-release support — flag for alpha/beta releases
  • Dry-run mode — preview before creating

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Auto-version + auto-changelog
python3 scripts/release.py

# Specific version
python3 scripts/release.py --version v1.2.0

# Pre-release
python3 scripts/release.py --version v1.0.0-beta.1 --prerelease

# Custom title + notes
python3 scripts/release.py --version v1.0.0 --title "First Stable Release" --notes "..."

# Preview without creating
python3 scripts/release.py --dry-run

# Just suggest a version
python3 scripts/release.py --suggest-version

docs.title: Changelog Format
docs.text:
Commits are grouped by conventional prefix:

Prefix Category feat: ✨ Features fix: 🐛 Bug Fixes docs: 📝 Documentation refactor: ♻️ Refactoring perf: ⚡ Performance test: ✅ Tests chore: 🔧 Chores security: 🛡️ Security

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• gh CLI installed and authenticated (gh auth login)
  • Git repository with remote configured

docs.title: Security Scan (scan.py)
docs.text: Prevents accidental secret leaks before publishing.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Detects 10+ secret patterns: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, AWS, Stripe, GitHub tokens
  • Validates .gitignore covers critical files (.env, secrets.toml, etc.)
  • Safe snippet display — only shows first/last 4 chars of found secrets
  • Exit code 1 on findings (CI-friendly)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
python3 scripts/scan.py
python3 scripts/scan.py --dir /path/to/project

docs.title: Detected Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Example OpenAI Key sk-... (48 chars) Anthropic Key sk-ant-... (93 chars) Google API Key AIza... (35 chars) AWS Access Key AKIA... (16 chars) Stripe Secret Key sk_live_... (24 chars) GitHub PAT ghp_... (36 chars) Firebase URL *.firebaseapp.com Generic API Key api_key = "..."

docs.title: CLI Recording (VHS)
docs.text: Terminal demo GIFs using VHS from Charmbracelet.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Tape file — Define commands in a .tape file
  2. VHS execution — VHS spawns a virtual terminal, "types" commands, records output
  3. GIF output — High-quality GIF sized for GitHub READMEs

docs.title: Template
docs.text:
See scripts/record_cli.tape for a working template:

Output showcase/cli_demo.gif
Set FontSize 22
Set Width 1200
Set Height 600

Type "pip install my-tool" Enter
Sleep 1s
Type "my-tool --help" Enter
Sleep 2s

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: vhs scripts/record_cli.tape

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Sleep intervals: Give viewers time to read output (1-2s between commands)
  • Font size: 22+ for mobile readability
  • Dimensions: 1200x600 fits READMEs well
  • Keep it short: 5-10 seconds max. Trim loading with Sleep.
